exemplar.title: To what extent has the merger of Kraft Food Group and H.J Heinz impacted  the performance of The Kraft Heinz Company in the U.S. Food & Beverage industry between 2018 to 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has the merger of Kraft Food Group and H.J Heinz impacted  the performance of The Kraft Heinz Company in the U.S. Food & Beverage industry between 2018 to 2023?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated clearly and effectively, with a concise background situating the merger context and objectives.
The research question is sharply focused, explicitly stated, and consistently aligned with the analysis.
A wide range of relevant secondary sources and analytical frameworks are selected with clear rationale.
weaknesses: Minor lapses in wording and occasional redundant sentences interrupt flow.
Methodology lacks detail on search strategy (databases, keywords) and integration of acknowledged limitations.
No primary research is conducted to complement secondary data.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place performance firmly in the 5â6 band across topic communication, question focus, and methodology. Minor lapses prevent full marks, so a score of 5/6 accurately reflects strong, wellâfocused work with room for methodological rigor improvements.

strengths: Concepts (e.g., cost synergies, zeroâbased budgeting, P/E ratio) are used accurately and consistently.
Source material (financial ratios, market data) is applied coherently to support discussion.
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, linking theory to the merger context effectively.
weaknesses: Some quantitative data lack precise citation, and a few competitor descriptions lack inâtext sourcing.
Application of tools (Ansoff Matrix, VRIO) is sometimes superficial and could deepen linkage to real initiatives.
Typographical errors in formulas and occasional redundant phrasing distract from clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands reach the top band (5), and one strand is solidly in the 3â4 band; overall performance is closer to 5/6. The work displays clear understanding and correct terminology but has minor citation and depth issues, justifying a 5/6.

strengths: Research draws on multiple years of audited statements, industry reports, and scholarly articles all relevant to the question.
Analysis of financial trends is wellâsupported by data and linked to strategic decisions.
Arguments are reasoned and evidenceâbased, with some evaluation of limitations in the conclusion.
weaknesses: Reliance solely on secondary data limits depth; no primary insights are offered.
Some sections drift into description rather than critical evaluation, lacking alternative explanations.
Referencing weaknesses and limited synthesis across studies temper the critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While research quality and analysis approach excellence, shortcomings in primary evidence, deeper synthesis, and fully critical evaluation prevent placement in the 10â12 bandâs highest echelon. A score of 10/12 reflects strong, relevant research and clear analysis with recognized areas for deeper critique.

strengths: Structure follows IB conventions (title page, contents, headings, conclusion, references).
Graphs, tables, and formulae are neatly inserted; word count and consistent font choices are provided.
weaknesses: Inconsistent pagination in the table of contents and broken URLs disrupt navigation.
Typographical errors (e.g., in formulas) and uneven figure sizing affect polish.
Overly detailed subâheadings clutter the contents page; formatting of inline LaTeX is incorrect.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and layout meet requirements clearly (3â4 band) but contain enough formatting and consistency errors to prevent the top mark. A score of 3/4 accurately captures correct but imperfect presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question appears verbatim on the cover page and is repeated inside the essay.  It is sharply delimited by company, type of strategic move, performance dimension, geography and five-year period, showing strong focus.  Throughout the paper the student refers back to this question when selecting tools (Ansoff, VRIO, ratios) and when drawing conclusions, demonstrating effective alignment.  There is slight drift where global data are occasionally introduced, so full marks are not justified, but the connection remains generally firm, meriting the upper band.

Arguments about cost-saving versus cultural strain, or liquidity recovery post-COVID, are reasoned and reference evidence.  Some evaluation of limitations appears in the conclusion.  However, critical weighing of alternative explanations is limited and synthesis across sections is moderate, so quality aligns with the middle of the 4-6 range.

The student demonstrates clear understanding of finance ratios, Ansoff Matrix, VRIO and Force-Field Analysis, applying them coherently to Kraft Heinz.  Explanations show accurate conceptual grasp and link theory to the merger context.  Minor inaccuracies (e.g., wording of some formulas) and limited critical depth keep the strand from a 6, but knowledge is organised and effective, justifying 5.

Business terminology such as âcost synergiesâ, âzero-based budgetingâ, âmarket penetrationâ, âacid-test ratioâ and âP/E ratioâ is used correctly and consistently.  Occasional typographical errors do not impede comprehension.  Overall precision and consistency align with the top band.

The student analyses trends (gross profit, liquidity, P/E) and links them to strategic decisions, providing evidence-based interpretations.  The analysis is clear and usually supported by data, though it sometimes drifts into description (e.g., extended narrative of promotional campaigns) and lacks counter-arguments, capping performance at the top of the 4-6 band.

Data, figures and quotations are generally applied in contextâe.g., using 2023 market capitalisation to discuss industry standing, or Statista employee numbers to support an HR argument.  However, some citations are second-hand or lack depth of integration, and occasional statistics are discussed only briefly.  This places the work solidly in the âmostly relevant and appropriateâ band rather than the highest level.

A wide range of secondary sources is signposted (annual reports, Statista, academic cases, Forbes, SEC filings) and multiple analytical frameworks are chosen with an explicit rationale.  Charts and ratio calculations indicate informed source selection and extraction of relevant quantitative data.  A few references are blog-like or poorly formatted, and there is no evidence of primary research, so the work does not fully exemplify outstanding methodology, but the breadth and appropriateness comfortably reach the 5-6 descriptor.

The investigation draws on multiple years of audited financial statements, industry reports, scholarly articles and strategic frameworks, all clearly related to the impact question.  Source diversity and relevance exceed the âadequateâ level and approach excellence; nonetheless, reliance on secondary data alone and some referencing weaknesses suggest an 8 rather than 10-12.

Graphs, tables and formulae are neatly inserted and the student provides a word count and consistent font choices.  Minor formatting issues (broken URLs, uneven figure sizing) prevent full marks, but overall layout considerations are applied correctly, justifying a 3 within the 3-4 band.

The student states the merger topic, time frame (2018-2023) and industry context explicitly in both the title block and the introduction, then provides concise historical background on each legacy firm and their competitive environment.  Key drivers, objectives, figures and competitors are included, giving the reader a clear, accurate and sufficiently detailed picture of what will be investigated.  Minor lapses in wording and a few redundant sentences keep the work from being exemplary, but overall communication and explanation are consistently clear and complete, warranting placement in the 5-6 band.

The essay follows IB EE conventions (title page, contents, introduction, main body with sub-headings, conclusion and references).  All required components are present, but some headings are overly detailed and pagination in the table of contents is inconsistent.  Thus, structure is clearly appropriate but not flawless, meriting the lower half of the top band.

annotations.comment: The student correctly provides a word count of 3923, demonstrating adherence to the 4000-word limit requirement.
The research question is clearly stated, sharply delimited by company, timeframe and industry, and consistently aligned with the analysis.
The cover page lists only "Topic: Merger" without a specific title of investigation. Include a full, descriptive title for clarity.
The table of contents includes overly detailed sub-headings with inconsistent pagination. Simplify headings and verify page numbers.
The introduction offers concise background on both legacy firms, clearly situating the merger context and objectives.
Several competitor descriptions lack in-text citations. Include sources for market share claims (e.g., NestlÃ©, Unilever data).
The objective paragraph on cost synergies provides data but omits a direct citation for the $1.5 billion figure. Add precise sourcing.
Quantitative data on market capitalization ($44 billion) effectively supports industry positioning.
Figure 1 is well-placed but the in-text discussion does not critically assess source credibility. Evaluate GrandViewâs methodology.
The final aim statement is repeated here. Integrate the research question only once and use this space for methodological justification.
The literature review paraphrases sources but sometimes lacks depth of synthesis. Critically compare Rajesh Kumar (2018) with contrary studies.
The inline LaTeX tag around â3G.â is formatted as plain text. Use proper math syntax: $$	ext{3G}$$ or plain text.
Methodology outlines multiple tools but omits search strategy detail (databases, keywords, selection criteria). Add to strengthen rigor.
The admission that external market effects are ignored is important, but this limitation should be integrated into the methodology rationale.
Section headings follow a clear hierarchy (h3, h4), aiding navigation through findings and analysis.
The discussion of IBM outsourcing is descriptive but lacks evaluation of potential supply-chain risks or long-term cost impact.
Attributing revenue increases solely to debt refinance overlooks marketing and product innovation factors. Incorporate alternative explanations.
The application of Ansoffâs Matrix is superficial; deepen by mapping specific growth strategies to actual KHC initiatives with data.
When describing promotional campaigns, link spend to ROI metrics (e.g., marketing ROI, sales uplift) rather than only volume.
Figure 3âs market share data are informative but lack critical synthesis; compare KHCâs share changes with competitor strategies.
Redundancy in the VRIO text (repeating âIn terms of rarityâ¦â) distracts. Consolidate into a single, coherent point.
The force-field analysis section introduces the tool well but does not unpack the figure before its appearance. Add a lead-in interpretation.
The GPM and NPM formulas are correct but the source of each formula should be cited (e.g., financial accounting textbook).
Inventory context in the acid test discussion is absent; explain how changes in stock levels influenced the ratio.
Table Iâs title spans two lines and table headers are inconsistent. Consolidate the description into a single clear title above the table.
Negative growth rates in early years are presented but not interpreted in depth; provide discussion on integration costs driving these values.
A typographical error in the P/E formula (â&41.32â) should be corrected to the proper numeric value.
The HR management section relies solely on secondary sources; consider adding a brief reflective discussion on potential primary interview insights.
The conclusion repeats earlier analysis rather than offering a concise synthesis of key findings. Focus on summarizing insights and implications.
Reference list shows inconsistent citation styles and broken URLs. Standardize to a single format (e.g., APA) and fix link breaks.
